title: Name-only process trust can suppress scanning, threat posture, and SOAR for a renamed executable
impact: A process renamed to an operator-trusted basename can bypass memory scanning and have process-tagged HIGH/CRITICAL events excluded from posture and automated response.
exploitability: Requires a pre-existing operator-approved name-only entry; exact-path Proton entries are not affected.
remediation: Require exact canonical path plus publisher/hash for suppression; keep name-only entries as non-enforcing display hints and migrate existing basename rows to review-required state.

title: AAR fuzzy PID/path matching can credit unrelated detections and remediation to drill steps
impact: Unrelated telemetry or one SOAR event can inflate detection/remediation results and hide real drill gaps.
exploitability: Can occur during ordinary runs through PID reuse/shared PID, repeated basenames, or later-step events; an in-process producer could also manufacture a false pass.
remediation: Propagate opaque run_id and step_id fields through markers, detector events, and SOAR actions; bound each step window and treat legacy PID/path matches as low-confidence only.

title: Newest-row-only retention lets telemetry volume evict critical audit evidence
impact: High-volume INFO/LOW traffic can remove earlier critical evidence and SOAR actions, damaging incident forensics and delayed AAR accuracy.
exploitability: Scanner/drill storms are sufficient; an adversary able to induce telemetry volume can accelerate eviction.
remediation: Use severity- and incident-aware tiered retention with a hard byte ceiling; prune INFO first and retain bounded signed critical/AAR evidence with visible retirement records.

title: Emergency shutdown terminates unrelated Python jobs and every Ollama model runner
impact: The elevated recovery script can kill unrelated Python applications and unload models used by other local applications, risking data loss.
exploitability: Requires an operator to invoke the emergency recovery script.
remediation: Track and validate Angerona-owned PIDs; command-line filter only as fallback; require a separate explicit force-all action before killing every Ollama runner.

title: ARIA's off switch is stored but not enforced by the assistant action gate
impact: After future tool registration, a disabled ARIA instance could still invoke reads and stage/confirm writes, violating the documented opt-out boundary.
exploitability: Dormant today because the combined app does not yet wire ARIA tools; becomes active immediately when integration registers callbacks.
remediation: Fail closed in invoke/confirm/proactive/voice paths while disabled, clear pending tokens on disable, bind confirmation to immutable action identity, and add zero-callback disabled-state tests.
